items.source: SF Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c=false; no Contact Role above Evaluator; zero Avoma calls
SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator only); zero Avoma calls
SF Competitors__c named with no supporting call, quote, or Next Step context
SF What_is_the_decision_process__c and Business_Requirements__c both blank; zero Avoma calls
Only 2 buyer-side Contact Roles on the opportunity, both Evaluators

metrics.narrative: Value_to_Customer__c states Zycus can help Monroe standardize sourcing, automate bid comparison, improve procurement governance, and reduce manual effort across sourcing and purchasing, but no figure (cost, headcount, cycle time) has ever been quantified. This read comes entirely from Salesforce free text (recorded 18 May 2026); no call has occurred to press Monroe for a number. Quantifying the impact of moving off a fully manual process is the move, not an assumption.

sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (3, 11, 19, 29 Jun 2026, no reply logged)
champion.narrative: No Contact Role above Evaluator exists for Nona MCIPS or Maria Camarre, and no advocacy (introductions, internal document sharing, pushing for Zycus) has been logged. Both contacts have gone unresponsive to three follow-up attempts since 3 Jun 2026, which is the opposite of active championing. There is no champion on this deal today.

competition.narrative: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and JAGGAER are named in Competitors__c, but zero call, quote, or Next Step context explains how they were identified, whether Monroe is actively evaluating all three, or how Zycus is perceived against them. Why_not_Zycus__c flags a real risk: as Monroe's first move into procurement automation, it may default to a cheaper point solution instead. The wedge is combined sourcing and procurement in one platform (Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), but this has never been tested against Monroe's actual criteria.

identify_pain.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c states plainly: current sourcing and procurement processes are fully manual, with no system in place, and Monroe wants to support a structured three bids to buy procurement process (recorded 18 May 2026). This is real, confirmed pain, but it comes from a Salesforce free-text field rather than a buyer quote on a call, and the pain owner (likely Nona MCIPS given her Procurement Leader title) has not been confirmed as such on any call.

paper_process.narrative: Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank and no MSA, SOW, or legal-review signal exists, which is expected at Qualified stage this early in a new-business motion. Paper process visibility will only emerge once commercials and an RFP round are underway; nothing to flag as a near-term concern yet.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false, Who_will_approve_budget__c is blank, and both Opportunity Contact Roles (Nona MCIPS, Maria Camarre) carry the Evaluator role, not budget authority. No call has occurred to surface who controls the spend. The gap is genuine, not just a hygiene issue: the move is to ask Nona or Maria directly who signs off on a first-time procurement-automation purchase once contact resumes.

decision_process.narrative: What_is_the_decision_process__c and Mandate__c are blank, and no call sequence exists to read escalation or approval patterns from. Only two mid-level Evaluator contacts have ever engaged, and even they have gone silent since 18 May 2026, so the process Monroe is actually running is unknown.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c and Top_Challenges_Priorities__c are both blank, and no call, RFI, or workshop has occurred to surface evaluation criteria. All that is known is the high-level ask: a structured three-bids-to-buy process across sourcing and procurement. Getting the RFP released is the only path to real criteria.

items.name: Opportunity Health: process/evaluation stage unclear
items.summary: System flags that we lack clarity on the buyer's evaluation stages and haven't secured influence over the process — a structural qualification gap, not a completed call.

items.name: Opportunity Health: org chart / contact mapping needed
items.summary: Flags that the key contacts and org structure at Monroe Energy still aren't mapped, leaving us blind on who else matters in the account.

items.name: Opportunity Health: APD review pending
items.summary: Flags an outstanding need to review and approve APD information — an internal step that hasn't been closed out yet.

items.name: Opportunity Health: low inbound from prospect
items.summary: System flags low inbound communication from Monroe Energy, reinforcing that the buyer side has gone quiet.

items.name: Opportunity Health: no decision maker identified
items.summary: A core risk flag — despite time in this deal, no confirmed economic/decision maker has been identified, which puts the whole opportunity's validity in question.

items.name: Opportunity Health: multi-threading needed
items.summary: Flags that engagement is too narrow and urges spreading contact across more stakeholders at Monroe Energy.

items.name: Opportunity Health: more contact roles needed
items.summary: Reinforces the multi-threading gap — asks for identification of additional roles/contacts beyond whoever is currently engaged.

items.name: Opportunity Health: prospect gone cold
items.summary: Flags zero inbound from the prospect in the last 14 days, confirming the deal has stalled from the buyer's side.

items.name: Opportunity Health: 10-day outbound gap
items.summary: Automated nudge noting our own team hasn't sent outbound email in 10 days, prompting a reminder to send follow-ups — reply/engagement from Monroe Energy is still pending.

items.name: Nooks call task: Nona MCIPS, Step #6 Quack US-ME DDs
items.summary: A scheduled/logged call task to reach Nona (MCIPS) as part of step 6 of a due-diligence sequence tied to the US-ME (Monroe Energy) deal, likely a dialer-generated task rather than a completed substantive conversation.

day_summary.overall: On the most recent active day, the Monroe Energy deal saw no genuine buyer engagement — instead, a batch of automated Opportunity Health flags fired, all pointing to the same underlying problem: this deal has stalled and is under-qualified. No inbound from the prospect in 14 days, no decision maker identified, and outbound email has gone quiet for 10 days. We're entirely on our side of the net now, needing to rebuild multi-threading and get basic process/contact clarity before this can move again.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: No real champion yet — No champion exists on this deal. Both known contacts (Nona MCIPS, Maria Camarre) hold the Evaluator role only, with no documented advocacy, and both have gone silent since 18 May 2026 despite four rep follow-ups.
bullets.text: No real champion yet — No champion exists on this deal.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning is intentionally not the lead here: Monroe's stated need is basic sourcing/procurement governance, not AI capability, so a Negative AIS reflects the buyer's real ask rather than a Zycus positioning failure.
bullets.text: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning is intentionally not the lead here: Monroe's stated need is basic sourcing/procurement governance, not AI capability, so a Negative AIS reflects…

bullets.full: No decisive edge over the field yet — Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero competitive intelligence. No discovery call, RFP, or buyer engagement to explain how these were identified, whether Monroe is actively evaluating all three, or what their positioning is. Zycus's differentiation wedge—Merlin's agentic AI negotiation (I2O: Insight to Outcome, autonomous supplier price optimization)—has not been presented to the buyer. Competitive position is completely blind. High risk that Monroe has already shortlisted another vendor and is using Zycus for price benchmarking or compliance.
bullets.text: No decisive edge over the field yet — Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero competitive intelligence.

cro_panel.header: Monroe Energy, LLC — Qualified · $100K · closes 21 Jul

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 12 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 5 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: momentum 25 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 25 (slipping backward): no activity instrumentation on this record yet (cadence reads fully after its next sweep) — Salesforce shows activity 8d ago. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.47).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 50 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 7 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (5), nudged by commitment 50 and momentum 25, and dragged by risk 12. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: no activity instrumentation on this record yet (cadence reads fully after its next sweep) — Salesforce shows activity 8d ago

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled for roughly seven weeks: three rep outreach attempts since 3 Jun 2026 have gone unanswered and no discovery call has ever taken place.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 May 2026, moved to Qualified 18 May 2026 (Qualified_Submission_Date__c), so the opportunity has sat about 50 days in Qualified as of 7 Jul 2026. Close date is 45 days out. No Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c or Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c exist, meaning zero progression past Qualified. A realistic enterprise P2P and sourcing motion (RFP response, shortlist, demos, workshops, commercials, legal) needs 4 to 6 months from RFP release alone, and the RFP has not been released. The deal cannot reach Closed Won by 21 Aug 2026 on current pace.
evidence: Next_Step_History__c shows the identical unresolved RFP-release ask repeated 3 Jun, 11 Jun, 19 Jun, 29 Jun 2026
Zero Avoma meetings found across opp, account, and attendee-email discovery (2025-01-01 to 2026-07-07)
north_star_verdict.headline: Stalled in Qualified for about 50 days with zero confirmed buyer reply since 18 May 2026; the 21 Aug 2026 close date is not achievable without a discovery call opening in the next two weeks.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Hold at Pipeline and push the close date to at least Q4 2026 or Q1 2027 until a discovery call and RFP release are confirmed with the buyer.

flags: Deal is cold (zero buyer calls, 24 days since last SF activity, rep outreach 19 Jun unanswered). Re-engagement is the only path forward. If Nona and Maria do not reply within 5 business days (by 3 Jul), escalate to the Procurement Lead or CFO level to restart momentum. Without a discovery call by early Jul, the 21 Aug close is impossible and the deal should be disqualified or pushed to Q4.

Competitive intelligence is a complete gap. Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER) but no buyer statements, no shortlist confirmation, no threat ranking. GEP and Coupa are both Tier-1 and can win on P2P + sourcing; JAGGAER is procurement-native. Merlin AI is the wedge but buyer AI appetite is unconfirmed. Immediate move: extract the shortlist, buyer preferences, and each rival's positioning on the discovery call. Then run a competitive deep-dive (battle cards, win themes, proof points) within 14 days and position Merlin as the differentiator in every subsequent conversation.

No champion: Nona and Maria are Evaluators with zero engagement. No Champion, Decision Maker, Economic Buyer, or Executive Sponsor identified in contact roles. No internal advocate to guide Zycus through Monroe's process or defend against the three named competitors (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER). Cannot navigate approval chain or internal politics without a champion.

No Economic Buyer access: EB—the executive who controls budget and signs the contract—is unidentified. Contact roles list only two Evaluators (Nona, Maria). Zero visibility into who holds budget authority, whether funds are allocated, or whether this is an approved project vs market research. Cannot validate deal viability or commercial terms without EB engagement.

Single-threaded, no multi-threading: Only two contacts (Nona MCIPS, Maria Camarre, both Evaluators), both unresponsive. No senior stakeholders (VP Procurement, CFO, Operations exec) mapped. Zero Avoma calls, zero tasks/events with additional buyer participants. Single-thread stall: if Nona and Maria disengage or leave, deal dies.

The single live human connection (positive call with Maria Camarre, 18 May 2026) is now ~7 weeks stale with no follow-up meeting booked; convert it to a scheduled discovery call before it goes fully cold.

The RFP-release ask has been repeated four times (3, 11, 22, 30 Jun 2026) with no written response; press for a specific date rather than repeating the same open-ended ask, and use the answer to gauge whether Monroe is genuinely running a live tender or has deprioritized the project.
Correct the forecast honestly: a 21 Aug 2026 close date sitting atop zero discovery call, RFP, or demo is not credible and should be reset before it slips silently.
best_practice_check.summary: This is an early-stage, single-thread deal where the highest-leverage moves are reopening contact, mapping the competitive field before it is chosen blind, and correcting a close date that predates any real evaluation activity.

competitive_position.summary: Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero competitive intelligence. No discovery call, RFP, or buyer engagement to explain how these were identified, whether Monroe is actively evaluating all three, or what their positioning is. Zycus's differentiation wedge—Merlin's agentic AI negotiation (I2O: Insight to Outcome, autonomous supplier price optimization)—has not been presented to the buyer. Competitive position is completely blind. High risk that Monroe has already shortlisted another vendor and is using Zycus for price benchmarking or compliance.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin agentic negotiation (I2O: supplier price intelligence, autonomous RFP optimization on historical data) vs GEP's consulting-heavy, less-mature AI. Position Merlin as Phase 1 centerpiece in RFP response and demo.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin agentic negotiation + deeper sourcing AI vs Coupa's broad but AI-shallow platform. Emphasize I2O autonomy and supplier collaboration depth in discovery and demo.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: Merlin agentic negotiation + modern UX vs JAGGAER's legacy platform and weaker AI. Position Merlin as the AI differentiation in RFP and use-case demos.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is intentionally not the lead here: Monroe's stated need is basic sourcing/procurement governance, not AI capability, so a Negative AIS reflects the buyer's real ask rather than a Zycus positioning failure.

items.quote: Current sourcing and procurement processes are fully manual, with no system in place.
items.criterion: Move from fully manual sourcing/procurement to a structured three bids to buy process

items.quote: Zycus can support both sourcing and procurement modules in one platform, helping Monroe move from manual processes to structured RFx, bid comparison, approvals, and guided buying workflows.
items.criterion: Single platform covering both sourcing and procurement

items.quote: There might be a chance for them to consider point solutions or cheaper alternatives since it will be their first step into procurement automation.
items.criterion: Risk of choosing a cheaper point solution as a first-time automation buyer

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Where Monroe's stated need is known, Zycus scope aligns well (combined sourcing and procurement replacing a fully manual process). The one clear exposure is that, as a first-time procurement-automation buyer, Monroe could default to a cheaper point solution instead of a full platform.

hard.ais_why:
* Monroe Energy is starting from zero: "Current sourcing and procurement processes are fully manual, with no system in place," and it wants to "support a structured three bids to buy procurement process."
* The objective is basic sourcing control and governance for an oil-and-gas refiner, not AI capability.
* Next step is scheduling a project/modules demo; Merlin is attached to a core iSource/eProcurement scope but AI is not part of the stated need. Qualified at 10%.

deltas.subject: The single live human connection (positive call with Maria Camarre, 18 May 2026) is now ~7 weeks stale with no follow-up meeting booked; convert it to a scheduled discovery call before it goes fully cold.

deltas.subject: Zero access to power: both known contacts are Evaluators with no budget authority; open a second thread toward a senior procurement or finance stakeholder to get any read on economic buyer identity.

deltas.to: The RFP-release ask has been repeated four times (3, 11, 22, 30 Jun 2026) with no written response; press for a specific date rather than repeating the same ope
deltas.subject: The RFP-release ask has been repeated four times (3, 11, 22, 30 Jun 2026) with no written response; press for a specific date rather than repeating the same open-ended ask, and use the answer to gauge whether Monroe is genuinely running a live tender or has deprioritized the project.

Multi-threading gap: only 2 buyer-side contact roles (Nona, Maria, both Evaluators), no champion, no EB, no DM. Monroe is a $100k enterprise deal requiring C-level or VP approval, but no senior stakeholders are mapped. Add Procurement Lead (account contact roster shows a Procurement Lead, not on opp roles), CFO (Matthew O'Mahoney or Patrick Callan, data unclear), and COO (Regan Howell) to the opportunity and reach them in parallel during or immediately after discovery. Single-threading to two unresponsive mid-level evaluators is a ghost risk.

Zero buyer engagement: Nona MCIPS and Maria Camarre (the only two contacts, both Evaluators) have not responded to three rep-initiated outreach attempts—3 Jun (discovery call request), 11 Jun (RFP follow-up), 19 Jun (LinkedIn connect). No discovery call, no demos, no RFP received. LastActivityDate 2 Jun 2026 is a Salesforce-logged activity, not a two-way buyer conversation. 28 days since last activity, 11 days since most recent rep outreach (19 Jun). Ghost risk is high.

Close date not credible: 52 days to close (21 Aug 2026) with zero buyer engagement, no discovery, no demos, no RFP, no shortlist, no champion, no EB. A realistic enterprise P2P + sourcing motion (RFP response, vendor shortlist, demos, use-case validation, commercials, legal review, contracting) requires 4 to 6 months, not 8 weeks. Close date was set at opportunity creation (15 May) and has never been validated with the buyer. Forecast category is Pipeline (correct—deal is not progressing), but the close date should be pushed to Q1 2027 or the deal should be disqualified.

deltas.subject: Zero buyer engagement despite three rep outreach attempts (3 Jun, 11 Jun, 19 Jun). Nona MCIPS and Maria Camarre (the only two contacts, both Evaluators) are unresponsive. deltas.subject: Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero competitive intelligence. deltas.to: No competitive intelligence: Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero context—how identified, where in eval process, what their positio
deltas.subject: No competitive intelligence: Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero context—how identified, where in eval process, what their positioning is, how Zycus should differentiate. No discovery call or RFP to provide competitive framing. High risk that Monroe has already shortlisted another vendor and is using Zycus for price benchmarking. Competitive position is completely blind.

56 days to close (21 Aug) with zero buyer engagement, no discovery call, no demos, no RFP, no champion, no EB access. A realistic enterprise motion (RFP response, shortlist, demos, workshops, commercials, legal, contract) requires 4-6 months, not 8 weeks. Close date is not credible.

Zero buyer engagement despite rep outreach 3 Jun, 11 Jun, 19 Jun. Nona and Maria (the only two buyer contacts) are unresponsive. No champion, no EB access, no senior stakeholder mapped. Deal may be a low-priority internal project, or Monroe may be favoring another vendor and ghosting Zycus. Ghost risk is high.

Three competitors (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER) named but zero competitive intelligence. Buyer may have already shortlisted or selected another vendor and is going through the motions with Zycus for compliance or price benchmarking. Competitive position is completely blind.

Deal is cold (zero buyer calls, 24 days since last SF activity, rep outreach 19 Jun unanswered). Re-engagement is the only path forward. If Nona and Maria do not reply within 5 business days (by 3 Jul), escalate to the Procurement Lead or CFO level to restart momentum. Without a discovery call by early Jul, the 21 Aug close is impossible and the deal should be disqualified or pushed to Q4.

Multi-threading gap: only 2 buyer-side contact roles (Nona, Maria, both Evaluators), no champion, no EB, no DM. Monroe is a $100k enterprise deal requiring C-level or VP approval, but no senior stakeholders are mapped. Add Procurement Lead (account contact roster shows a Procurement Lead, not on opp roles), CFO (Matthew O'Mahoney or Patrick Callan, data unclear), and COO (Regan Howell) to the opportunity and reach them in parallel during or immediately after discovery. Single-threading to two unresponsive mid-level evaluators is a ghost risk.

Competitive intelligence is a complete gap. Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER) but no buyer statements, no shortlist confirmation, no threat ranking. GEP and Coupa are both Tier-1 and can win on P2P + sourcing; JAGGAER is procurement-native. Merlin AI is the wedge but buyer AI appetite is unconfirmed. Immediate move: extract the shortlist, buyer preferences, and each rival's positioning on the discovery call. Then run a competitive deep-dive (battle cards, win themes, proof points) within 14 days and position Merlin as the differentiator in every subsequent conversation.

value.flag: Zero buyer engagement: Nona MCIPS and Maria Camarre (the only two contacts, both Evaluators) have not responded to three rep-initiated outreach attempts—3 Jun (discovery call request), 11 Jun (RFP follow-up), 19 Jun (LinkedIn connect). No discovery call, no demos, no RFP received. LastActivityDate 2 Jun 2026 is a Salesforce-logged activity, not a two-way buyer conversation. 28 days since last activity, 11 days since most recent rep outreach (19 Jun). Ghost risk is high.

value.flag: No champion: Nona and Maria are Evaluators with zero engagement. No Champion, Decision Maker, Economic Buyer, or Executive Sponsor identified in contact roles. No internal advocate to guide Zycus through Monroe's process or defend against the three named competitors (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER). Cannot navigate approval chain or internal politics without a champion.

value.flag: No Economic Buyer access: EB—the executive who controls budget and signs the contract—is unidentified. Contact roles list only two Evaluators (Nona, Maria). Zero visibility into who holds budget authority, whether funds are allocated, or whether this is an approved project vs market research. Cannot validate deal viability or commercial terms without EB engagement.

value.flag: Single-threaded, no multi-threading: Only two contacts (Nona MCIPS, Maria Camarre, both Evaluators), both unresponsive. No senior stakeholders (VP Procurement, CFO, Operations exec) mapped. Zero Avoma calls, zero tasks/events with additional buyer participants. Single-thread stall: if Nona and Maria disengage or leave, deal dies.

value.flag: Close date not credible: 52 days to close (21 Aug 2026) with zero buyer engagement, no discovery, no demos, no RFP, no shortlist, no champion, no EB. A realistic enterprise P2P + sourcing motion (RFP response, vendor shortlist, demos, use-case validation, commercials, legal review, contracting) requires 4 to 6 months, not 8 weeks. Close date was set at opportunity creation (15 May) and has never been validated with the buyer. Forecast category is Pipeline (correct—deal is not progressing), but the close date should be pushed to Q1 2027 or the deal should be disqualified.

value.flag: No competitive intelligence: Three competitors named (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) with zero context—how identified, where in eval process, what their positioning is, how Zycus should differentiate. No discovery call or RFP to provide competitive framing. High risk that Monroe has already shortlisted another vendor and is using Zycus for price benchmarking. Competitive position is completely blind.
